reviews.review_msg: Had him for ENSC151, he had pretty good lectures and would occasionally do in class activities worth marks but attendance wasn't mandatory. Exams and labs were fair but TA's were completely clueless and often didn't know how to help students.

Lectures were generally organized and easy to follow. In class examples and practice were very helpful. Midterm and final exams were fair and pretty straightforward, the labs were much more difficult. Great prof however the TA's for the course were very unorganized and often didn't show up to labs at all.

He's a good guy, not necessarily the best or the most prepared prof out there but it was clear he was trying his best. The TAs weren't helpful, but the crap ton of homework he assigned us nailed the concepts in my head. For an introductory course in coding (C++), I found him great! You'll get lost and confused, but that's a part of learning!

He has good actual programming knowledge and he isn't a bad teacher but he's very unorganized. My biggest gripe however is with the TA's, many of which did not know how to do the assignments and labs we were assigned, and some of which did not even show up to their scheduled office hours. If you want to succeed in this course just self learn C++

Craig FORGOT to mark my grade for a group project worth roughly 10-15% of my final mark. When I confronted him about the mark, he told me either I write a whole new quiz, or I don't get credit for the mark...

Terrible classes, lectures are just updating his own project, meaning if you miss one you're screwed.

Take a course with Craig and you'll understand how important slide is for teaching. Without those slides, you literally forget the main point after a while and be confused. Also his test multiple choice gets really technical wording distinctions. He's a nice guy, tho I don't know how he would react if you skip lecture but go to office hour.
этот парень ужасен, кто позволил ему преподавать?

Craig's lectures can be a bit rambling, but he's a nice guy and generous with partial credit. Textbook is a must, and Unstuck ai proved helpful for C++ practice alongside the course materials. Overall, manageable with effort.

Professor Craig Scratchley has a unique teaching style, but his tendency to curve grades down can be discouraging. While this approach pushes students to work harder, it often feels unfair. With clearer explanations of tough concepts, his classes could be more effective and less frustrating for students.

Was not an enjoyable class. I got a 69.99% (keep in mind 70% is a B-) and asked if it could be rounded up and his response was basically along the lines of "No because if you were above the grade threshold you wouldn't ask for the lower grade so I can't raise your grade by 0.01%" and "If I did that for you I would have to for everyone".

Craig really shouldn't be teaching lower level courses. His lectures are kinda pointless to go to, he's just doing like trial and error stuff. The textbook he uses really isn't for beginners, but it's ok.
Although he's a bad prof, he's a very wholesome and nice human being. He's also really useful during office hours and during labs.

Rambles in lectures so it gets hard to follow along. He has zoom lectures recorded if you wanna skip. I suggest learning c++ through other websites and use Craig as a supporting role. Kind man and very hard to fail the class. Final looked VERY similar to midterm

Terrible. Just terrible. Terrible at teaching, terrible at grading, terrible at explaining. He is good at giving second chances, but thats about it. This is NOT a beginner friendly course. Skip his lectures and learn C++ on your own. Not worth attending. Awful prof, not sure how SFU still has him after so many valid complaints.

This class is on c++. Make sure you keep up with reading the textbook sections he covers in lectures. Beware of due dates for the 3 coding assignments, late hand-ins are docked 10% each day late. Craig and his TA's are generous markers, the lectures can be dull and unorganized, but you can easily get help from Craig and in the computer lab.

Lectures consist of um and uh. Just read the book. Assignment instructions are as clear as his lectures (not at all). His midterm and final are bizarre. *The only lectures worth watching are the ones right before the midterm and final.*

Craig please come up with lecture plans and clarify your instructions. Lectures could be so much more efficient.
